mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings. This positive economic momentum translates into positive equity returns over a short-term horizon as investors update their expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value is 135613.0, indicating a large workforce.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold of 5%, borrowing costs for corporations increase and the discount rate on future earnings rises. This compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 5.25, which is above the 5.0% threshold.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling real retail sales (RSAFS) and a high level of industrial production (INDPRO) signals an inventory build-up due to weakening consumer demand. This mismatch leads to future production cuts and lower earnings, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < -1% AND INDPRO is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: The two-factor signal, when triggered, is not followed by negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases during a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a primary indicator of a recession. When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE crosses above its 12-month moving average, it signals a structural shift in the labor market, negatively impacting investor sentiment and future growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month MA of UNRATE > 12-month MA of UNRATE
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 4.6, a key cyclical indicator.
failure_condition: In a walk-forward test, this signal trigger has a false positive rate (predicting a downturn that does not occur) greater than 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that the supply side of the economy is expanding robustly, meeting demand and supporting corporate revenue growth. This fundamental strength drives positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in CPI (CPIAUCSL) is decelerating while the Fed Funds rate remains high, it signals that monetary policy is becoming increasingly restrictive in real terms. This 'over-tightening' weighs on economic activity and leads to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: YoY % change in CPIAUCSL is decreasing AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting equities when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full monetary policy cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a slowdown in consumer spending and construction, two key components of GDP. This negative signal for the real economy leads to poor short-term equity performance as the market prices in lower growth.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the technical definition of an economic contraction. This confirmation of broad-based economic weakness causes a significant downward revision in earnings forecasts, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is a high revision-risk series.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a negative real GDP print, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 45% of the time, indicating the signal is already priced in.
